blocks.html: Time and day meet in this line. Night turns into morning before we walk again.
blocks.plain: Time and day meet in this line. Night turns into morning before we walk again.
blocks.plainEs: El tiempo y el día se encuentran en esta línea. La noche se vuelve mañana antes de volver a caminar.

blocks.html: I walk to the house and see the door. The window shows a little light inside the room.
blocks.plain: I walk to the house and see the door. The window shows a little light inside the room.
blocks.plainEs: Camino hasta la casa y veo la puerta. La ventana muestra una pequeña luz dentro de la habitación.

blocks.html: I think I know this place. My heart remembers the story I read in an old book.
blocks.plain: I think I know this place. My heart remembers the story I read in an old book.
blocks.plainEs: Creo que conozco este lugar. Mi corazón recuerda la historia que leí en un libro viejo.

blocks.html: Every word mattered to the child and the friend who shared the world of that tale.
blocks.plain: Every word mattered to the child and the friend who shared the world of that tale.
blocks.plainEs: Cada palabra importaba al niño y al amigo que compartieron el mundo de ese relato.

blocks.html: Life is short; love and hope can fill it. The sun, the moon, and one bright star marked the sky.
blocks.plain: Life is short; love and hope can fill it. The sun, the moon, and one bright star marked the sky.
blocks.plainEs: La vida es breve; el amor y la esperanza pueden llenarla. El sol, la luna y una estrella brillante marcaron el cielo.

blocks.html: We hear the wind and feel the sea. A ship once waited where the road meets the forest.
blocks.plain: We hear the wind and feel the sea. A ship once waited where the road meets the forest.
blocks.plainEs: Oímos el viento y sentimos el mar. Una vez un barco esperaba donde el camino se encuentra con el bosque.

blocks.html: Questions of hope
blocks.plain: Questions of hope
blocks.plainEs: Preguntas de esperanza

blocks.html: One simple question can change a year. In a quiet moment I asked for peace instead of fear.
blocks.plain: One simple question can change a year. In a quiet moment I asked for peace instead of fear.
blocks.plainEs: Una pregunta sencilla puede cambiar un año. En un momento tranquilo pedí paz en lugar de miedo.

blocks.html: I dream at night and wake by day. My voice was small, but the song still carried truth.
blocks.plain: I dream at night and wake by day. My voice was small, but the song still carried truth.
blocks.plainEs: Sueño de noche y despierto de día. Mi voz era pequeña, pero la canción aún llevaba verdad.

blocks.html: Love and life go together. Truth is better than any lie, good or bad.
blocks.plain: Love and life go together. Truth is better than any lie, good or bad.
blocks.plainEs: El amor y la vida van juntos. La verdad es mejor que cualquier mentira, buena o mala.

blocks.html: • People work, play, and learn when they have time.
blocks.plain: People work, play, and learn when they have time.
blocks.plainEs: La gente trabaja, juega y aprende cuando tiene tiempo.

blocks.html: • Teachers teach answers, yet the great question remains.
blocks.plain: Teachers teach answers, yet the great question remains.
blocks.plainEs: Los maestros enseñan respuestas, pero la gran pregunta sigue ahí.

blocks.html: The city and the road
blocks.plain: The city and the road
blocks.plainEs: La ciudad y el camino

blocks.html: Water and fire shaped the old city. A man and a woman walked the road toward home.
blocks.plain: Water and fire shaped the old city. A man and a woman walked the road toward home.
blocks.plainEs: El agua y el fuego moldearon la ciudad antigua. Un hombre y una mujer caminaron por el camino hacia casa.

blocks.html: The horse and the dog rested near a tree. A bird watched from a stone by the table.
blocks.plain: The horse and the dog rested near a tree. A bird watched from a stone by the table.
blocks.plainEs: El caballo y el perro descansaron cerca de un árbol. Un pájaro miraba desde una piedra junto a la mesa.

blocks.html: Bread and wine sat on the chair. Gold and stone tell different stories under the eye of the king.
blocks.plain: Bread and wine sat on the chair. Gold and stone tell different stories under the eye of the king.
blocks.plainEs: El pan y el vino estaban en la silla. El oro y la piedra cuentan historias distintas bajo la mirada del rey.

blocks.html: The queen spoke of war and peace. Young and old people listened in that public place.
blocks.plain: The queen spoke of war and peace. Young and old people listened in that public place.
blocks.plainEs: La reina habló de guerra y paz. Jóvenes y mayores escucharon en aquel lugar público.

blocks.html: Sleep came after a long day of work. I did not want death; I wanted another morning.
blocks.plain: Sleep came after a long day of work. I did not want death; I wanted another morning.
blocks.plainEs: El sueño llegó tras un largo día de trabajo. No quería la muerte; quería otra mañana.

blocks.html: The forest path was dark, but the city light returned. Name this year as you wish.
blocks.plain: The forest path was dark, but the city light returned. Name this year as you wish.
blocks.plainEs: El sendero del bosque estaba oscuro, pero la luz de la ciudad regresó. Nombra este año como quieras.

blocks.html: This short sample ends here. Use tap translation, replace mode, or word blend to test the reader.
blocks.plain: This short sample ends here. Use tap translation, replace mode, or word blend to test the reader.
blocks.plainEs: Esta breve muestra termina aquí. Usa la traducción al toque, el modo de reemplazo o la mezcla de palabras para probar el lector.
